subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Board & Tracks
- **1** main game board with 4 attribute tracks (Faith, Strength, Influence, Virtue)
- **1** round tracker (7 rounds)
- **Wall development area** on the board

#### Workers
- **Red workers** — represent military strength
- **Blue workers** — represent faith and religion
- **Green workers** — represent commerce and influence
- **Neutral (grey/brown) workers** — flexible, can be used as any color but less efficiently
- **Worker supply bags** — for random worker draws

#### Cards
- **12** Paladin cards (unique paladin abilities, drafted each round)
- **King's Order cards** — end-game scoring objectives
- **Outsider cards** — recruitable characters with bonuses
- **Monk cards** — commissionable for VP and abilities
- **Tavern cards** — provide resources or workers

#### Resources & Tokens
- **Provisions tokens**
- **Silver tokens**
- **Score markers** — 1 per player
- **Attribute markers** — 4 per player (one per track)
- **Debt cards** — negative VP
- **Fortification tokens**
- **Garrison tokens**

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
1. **Place the main board** — shows action spaces, 4 attribute tracks, wall area, and round track.
2. **Attribute tracks** — Faith, Strength, Influence, Virtue run along the edges. Each has 12 positions.
3. **Wall area** — Central area where fortification tokens are placed.
4. **Round track** — Mark round 1 of 7.
5. **King's Orders** — Draw and display end-game scoring cards.
6. **Outsider display** — Lay out recruitable outsider cards.
7. **Monk supply** — Stack monk cards by type.
8. **Tavern deck** — Shuffle and place face-down.
9. **Worker supply** — Sort workers by color into supply areas. Fill the draw bags.
10. **Paladin deck** — Shuffle paladin cards for drafting.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Each Player Receives
- **Starting workers** — a mix of red, blue, green, and neutral workers (exact count per rules)
- **4 attribute markers** — place each at starting position on Faith, Strength, Influence, and Virtue tracks
- **1 score marker** — place on 0 of VP track
- **Starting provisions and silver** — small amounts to begin
- **Player board** — shows personal action spaces and worker holding areas

#### Starting Conditions
- All attribute tracks start at their lowest position.
- No debt cards to begin.
- Players receive their starting hand of tavern cards.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
- **7 rounds** — The game is played over exactly 7 rounds.
- **Paladin draft** — At the start of each round, players draft a Paladin card that determines which workers are available that round.
- **Worker availability** — Your Paladin card plus any leftover workers from previous rounds form your worker pool.
- **First player** — Determined randomly; rotates each round.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Round Structure
Each of the **7 rounds** follows this sequence:

1. **Paladin Draft** — Each player selects a Paladin card. This card shows which colored workers you receive this round.
2. **Worker Phase** — Players take turns placing workers on action spaces to perform actions.
3. **Clean Up** — Resolve end-of-round effects, advance round marker.

#### Worker Placement
- On your turn, place **one or more workers** on an action space.
- **Colored workers** (red, blue, green) are more efficient on matching spaces.
- **Neutral workers** can go anywhere but often require more workers to achieve the same effect.
- Some spaces require **specific colors** — e.g., fortify requires red workers.
- You continue taking turns until all players pass (no more workers or choose to stop).

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Main Actions
- **Fortify** — Place red workers to add fortification tokens to the wall. Advances Strength track.
- **Garrison** — Place workers to man the wall. Scores VP based on garrison size.
- **Commission Monks** — Place blue workers to recruit monks. Monks provide ongoing abilities and end-game VP. Advances Faith track.
- **Recruit Outsiders** — Place green workers to recruit outsiders. Outsiders give immediate bonuses and end-game VP. Advances Influence track.
- **Develop** — Improve your player board or unlock new abilities.
- **Pray** — Advance on the Faith track using blue workers.
- **Attack** — Use red workers to advance Strength and gain combat bonuses.
- **Influence** — Use green workers to advance the Influence track.
- **Tavern** — Draw tavern cards for resources or workers.

#### Virtue Track
- Certain actions move you up or down on the Virtue track.
- Recruiting outsiders (foreign help) may lower Virtue.
- Commissioning monks raises Virtue.
- Virtue scores VP at end of game.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Three Worker Colors
- **Red** — Military. Used for fortifying, garrison, combat.
- **Blue** — Religious. Used for commissioning monks, prayer.
- **Green** — Commerce. Used for recruiting outsiders, influence.
- Actions are cheaper when using the **matching color**. Using off-color or neutral workers costs more.

#### Paladin Cards
- Each Paladin card shows a specific combination of colored workers.
- Choosing a Paladin defines your strategy for that round.
- Some Paladins give many red workers (military focus), others lean blue or green.
- Paladin cards also provide a **unique ability** for the round.

#### Debt
- Certain actions or inability to pay costs results in **debt cards**.
- Each debt card is **-3 VP** at end of game.
- Debt can be paid off with silver during the game.

#### King's Orders
- Public end-game objectives that all players can see.
- Provide bonus VP for meeting specific conditions (e.g., most fortifications, highest Faith).

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### End of Game
The game ends after **7 rounds** are completed.

#### Final Scoring
- **Faith track** — VP based on position (higher = more, up to ~12 VP).
- **Strength track** — VP based on position.
- **Influence track** — VP based on position.
- **Virtue track** — VP based on position.
- **Commissioned monks** — VP printed on each monk card.
- **Recruited outsiders** — VP printed on each outsider card.
- **King's Orders** — Bonus VP for meeting objectives.
- **Garrison** — VP earned during the game for garrisoning.
- **Debt** — Each debt card is **-3 VP**.

#### Tiebreaker
Player with more remaining silver wins ties.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
- **Read the King's Orders** — These define what's valuable this game. Align your strategy accordingly.
- **Draft Paladins that match your plan** — If you want military focus, draft Paladins with lots of red workers.
- **Advance at least 2 tracks** — Spreading too thin scores poorly; ignoring tracks entirely also wastes potential.
- **Commission early monks** — They provide ongoing abilities that compound over 7 rounds.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
- **Ignoring debt** — At -3 VP each, debt is devastating. Pay it off or avoid it.
- **Spreading across all 4 tracks equally** — Focus on 2-3 tracks that synergize with your Paladins and King's Orders.
- **Wasting neutral workers** — Neutral workers are flexible but inefficient. Use them to fill gaps, not as primary workers.
- **Not planning for round 7** — The last round is critical. Save strong Paladins or resources for a final push.
- **Drafting Paladins reactively** — Plan 2-3 rounds ahead based on what you need.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
- **Paladin selection** — The most important decision each round. Defines your available workers and ability.
- **Track focus** — Which 2-3 attribute tracks to prioritize.
- **Monks vs. outsiders** — Monks raise virtue and give ongoing effects. Outsiders give immediate value but may lower virtue.
- **When to garrison** — Garrisoning earns VP but uses workers you could spend elsewhere.
- **Debt management** — Taking calculated debt can free up actions, but you must pay it off before game end.
